metadata.description: Core regressor construction with event timing, amplitude scaling, and HRF integration

semantic_description.purpose: Validate regressor construction with event-related modeling, parameter validation, and HRF integration
semantic_description.algorithm:
The regressor function creates event-related regressors for fMRI modeling:
**Core Algorithm:** 1. Input validation and conversion: - onsets: Event onset times (seconds), must be non-negative finite values - hrf: HRF object for convolution (defaults to HRF_SPMG1) - duration: Event durations (seconds), must be non-negative - amplitude: Event amplitudes/scaling factors - span: Temporal window (seconds), must be positive - summate: Logical flag for response summation
2. Parameter recycling: - duration and amplitude recycled to match number of onsets - Validation that all parameters have compatible lengths
3. Event filtering: - Remove events with amplitude = 0 or NA - Track whether any/all events were filtered - Update onset, duration, amplitude vectors accordingly
4. HRF validation and processing: - Ensure HRF is valid HRF object using make_hrf() - Extract HRF span for final span calculation
5. Object construction: - Create Reg object with validated components - Set filtered_all attribute for empty regressor detection - Maintain proper class hierarchy: c("Reg", "list")
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**Event Processing:** - Onset times must be non-negative (causal events) - Duration must be non-negative (physical constraint) - Amplitude can be any finite value (positive/negative scaling) - Filtering preserves event order and relationships
**Parameter Relationships:** - length(duration) = length(amplitude) = length(onsets) after recycling - Filtered vectors maintain index correspondence - span ≥ max(duration) for proper convolution
**Object Structure:** - onsets: Vector of event times - duration: Vector of event durations - amplitude: Vector of event amplitudes - hrf: HRF object for convolution - span: Temporal window - summate: Summation flag - filtered_all: Boolean attribute for empty detection
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Empty onsets: Should create valid empty regressor - Single onset: Should handle properly with recycling - Zero amplitudes: Should be filtered out - NA amplitudes: Should be filtered out - Negative onsets: Should trigger error - Negative durations: Should trigger error - Mixed valid/invalid amplitudes: Should filter selectively - Parameter length mismatches: Should recycle properly

input.name: mixed_amplitudes
input.description: Mixed amplitudes including zeros and negatives

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: regressor(onsets, hrf=HRF_SPMG1, duration=0, amplitude=1, span=40, summate=TRUE) # Location: R/reg-constructor.R lines 14-100 (Reg constructor) and line 159 (regressor alias)
# Define HRF objects for testing HRF_GAMMA <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gamma(t), name = "gamma")
# Test basic construction basic_reg <- regressor(c(5, 15, 25, 35), duration = c(2, 3, 2, 4), amplitude = c(1, 1.5, 0.8, 1.2))
# Test parameter recycling recycled_reg <- regressor(c(5, 15, 25), duration = 2, amplitude = 1.5)
# Test event filtering - avoid NA values that cause validation errors filtered_reg <- regressor(c(5, 10, 15, 20), amplitude = c(1, 0, -0.5, 2))
# Test single event single_reg <- regressor(10, duration = 2, amplitude = 1.5)
# Test empty regressor empty_reg <- regressor(numeric(0))
# Test all filtered all_filtered_reg <- regressor(c(5, 10, 15), amplitude = c(0, 0, 0))
# Test custom HRF custom_hrf_reg <- regressor(c(5, 15), hrf = HRF_GAMMA)
# Test boundary cases boundary_reg <- regressor(c(0, 0.1, 1, 5))
# Test large number of events large_reg <- regressor(1:100)
